D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Brewster's Angle & Polarization by Reflection

find the incidence angle where reflected light becomes perfectly s-polarized

The p-polarized reflectance falls to zero at one special angle, where the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular. The reflected beam is then pure s-polarization.

Set the two indices and sweep the incidence angle. The crimson curve is R_p, the slate curve R_s. The gold line marks Brewster's angle where R_p vanishes.
